focus: CONSOLIDATION: convert validated active-vector and masking evidence into an implementation-ready acceptance spec for memory_embedding_reconcile().

graphEvents.label: memory_embedding_reconcile dry-run contract has three action buckets plus one masked-row diagnostic bucket
Apply order is vector-present success reconciliation before missing-vector retry reset
15,152 masked failed rows should be reconciled to success, not pruned, by embedding reconcile
Missing-vector provider failures are report-only by default

label: memory_embedding_reconcile dry-run schema should report 17326 / 0 / 0 / 15152 for the current DB
evidence: Action buckets: vector_present_status_stale=17326, missing_active_vector_retry_eligible=0, missing_active_vector_provider_failure=0; diagnostic overlap bucket failed_masked_by_newer_latest_path_anchor_row=15152.

label: Apply mode must reconcile vector-present rows to success before any missing-vector reset
evidence: Iteration 7 proved all current non-success rows have active vectors; retry retention runs before queue selection, so blind reset can re-park rows before embedding work.

label: Masked failed rows should be reconciled, not pruned, in this maintenance tool
evidence: The 15152 masked failed rows overlap vector_present_status_stale; pruning is a separate retention/dedup concern with different referential-safety requirements.

label: memory_embedding_reconcile must resolve and verify the active shard from runtime metadata before counting or mutating rows
Provider failures with no active vector are report-only unless a future explicit unsafe override is designed
failed_masked_by_newer_latest_path_anchor_row is a diagnostic overlap bucket, not a mutually exclusive action bucket

direction: prune masked failed rows inside memory_embedding_reconcile
reason: The validated evidence proves stale embedding status with active vectors, not safe deletion. Pruning needs a separate dedup/retention tool with referential checks.
